subject: Databases
title: The CAP Theorem
description: A fast breakdown of The CAP Theorem for builders.

hook.text: You can have Consistency, Availability, or Partition tolerance — but not all three. Pick two.

buildup.text: Consistency: every read sees the latest write. Availability: every request gets a response.

discovery.text: Partition tolerance: the system works even if network links between nodes break.

twist.text: In distributed systems, partitions always happen. So you're really choosing between consistency and availability.

climax.text: Banks choose consistency (never show wrong balances). Social feeds choose availability (show stale but fast).

punchline.text: CAP: pick two. Networks break, so choose wisely.

quiz.question: Why does the CAP theorem force a choice between consistency and availability?
options: Network partitions are inevitable, so you must sacrifice one of the other two
Databases can't store more than two types of data
Consistency and availability require different hardware
